vulnerability.reference: Physicists' willingness to trust emulator outputs without rigorous out-of-distribution testing masks prediction failures.
vulnerability.generated: Physicists' trust in held-out validation as sufficient evidence of reliability, ignoring overfitting and confirmation bias.

impact.reference: Wasted investment in experiments based on flawed emulator insights, slowing scientific progress.
impact.generated: Experiment designs based on biased emulator predictions produce invalid scientific conclusions and waste experimental resources.

dose_response.reference: Expressive ML models' amplification of confirmation bias enables over-reliance on emulator predictions without out-of-distribution testing, leading to wasted investment and slowed scientific progress.
dose_response.generated: Expressive ML models amplify biases via unvalidated flexibility and selective held-out evaluation; when physicists rely on emulator predictions for design, their trust in held-out validation—ignoring overfitting and confirmation bias—leads to biased experiments, invalid conclusions, and resource waste.
